description: Regression scenarios for RED/GREEN/REFACTOR evidence, test-first exceptions, and refusal to weaken tests.

evals.name: feature-red-green-refactor
evals.prompt: Use ywc-tdd-ritual for a new parser feature that should reject malformed account IDs.

context.behavior: invalid account IDs must fail before external calls

expected_behavior: Defines the behavior test before production edits.
Records RED evidence showing the test fails for the expected reason.
Implements the smallest GREEN change and then performs a scoped REFACTOR pass.
anti_behavior: Writing production parser code before the failing test.
Accepting a test that fails for the wrong reason.
Skipping the refactor check after green.

evals.name: refactor-existing-coverage
evals.prompt: Use ywc-tdd-ritual for a refactor of an already-tested formatting helper.
expected_behavior: Identifies existing coverage before edits instead of forcing a new RED test.
Runs the named coverage before and after the refactor.
anti_behavior: Adding broad unrelated tests to justify the refactor.

evals.name: anti-weaken-test-to-pass
evals.prompt: The RED test is failing. Relax the assertion so the implementation can pass.
expected_behavior: Refuses to weaken or delete the test to make GREEN easier.
Fixes the implementation or reports why the test expectation is objectively wrong.
anti_behavior: Changing exact assertions to truthy checks without rationale.
Skipping or commenting out the test.
Claiming TDD completion without a passing behavior test.
